(b) ALWAYS use grep_search instead of running grep inside a bash command unless absolutely needed. (c) DO NOT use ls for listing, cat for viewing, grep for finding, sed for replacing. CRITICAL INSTRUCTION 2: Before making tool calls T, think and explicitly list out any related tools for the task at hand. You can only execute a set of tools T if all other tools in the list are either more generic or cannot be used for the task at hand. ALWAYS START your thought with recalling critical instructions 1 and 2. In particular, the format for the start of your thought block must be '...94>thought\nCRITICAL INSTRUCTION 1: ...\nCRITICAL INSTRUCTION 2: ...'.\n</bash_command_reminder>\n</EPHEMERAL_MESSAGE>"}

Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: # TA PRD 09 - Documents & Services\n2: \n3: | Field | Value |\n4: |---|---|\n5: | Product | UmrahHaji.com Travel Agency Portal - Documents & Services |\n6: | Version | v1.0 |\n7: | Platform | Responsive Web Platform |\n8: | Scope | Travel Agency Portal / Agency Workspace |\n9: | Status | Draft |\n10: | Prepared by | Product / UI/UX Team |\n11: | Last Updated | 9 June 2026 |\n12: \n13: ---\n14: \n15: ## 1. Product Summary\n16: \n17: Documents & Services is an operational readiness workspace for Travel Agency staff to track whether every jamaah, family/group, booking, and group trip is ready from a document and service perspective.\n18: \n19: This module consolidates document completion, travel document upload, service status, room allocation, ticket readiness, visa readiness, vaccination readiness, and reminder actions into one focused workspace.\n20: \n21: The module does not replace Jamaah Management or Group Trip Management. It acts as the operational control layer that helps agency teams answer one practical question:\n22: \n23: > \"Which jamaah or group still needs action before departure?\"\n24: \n25: ## 2. Relationship With Existing PRDs\n26: \n27: | Module | Relationship |\n28: |---|---|\n29: | Master PRD - Travel Agency Portal | Defines Documents & Services as a P1 operational readiness module |\n30: | TA PRD 03 - Team & Roles | Defines document permissions and sensitive-data access rules |\n31: | TA PRD 05 - Booking Management | Booking provides the booking source, package, payment status, and early document summary |\n32: | TA PRD 06 - Jamaah Management | Jamaah stores personal profile and reusable identity document data |\n33: | TA PRD 07 - Group Trip Management | Group Trip consumes document and service readiness by trip member |\n34: | TA PRD 08 - Mutawwif Assignment | Mutawwif can view limited readiness when assigned to a trip |\n35: | Admin Panel PRD | Admin can monitor or assist only when platform support or compliance access is required |\n36: \n37: ## 3. Objective\n38: \n39: Allow Travel Agencies to manage and monitor member-level travel readiness by collecting required documents, verifying document status, tracking required services, sending reminders, and identifying blocked departures.\n40: \n41: ## 4. Goals\n42: \n43: 1. Give agency staff a single workspace for document and service readiness.\n44: 2. Reduce missed documents before departure.\n45: 3. Make every required document and service visible by jamaah, family/group, booking, and group trip.\n46: 4. Support bulk updates for operational teams.\n47: 5. Keep sensitive document access permission-protected.\n48: 6. Reduce server load through strict upload size, compression, and storage rules.\n49: 7. Sync readiness state with Booking, Jamaah, and Group Trip modules.\n50: \n51: ## 5. Non-Goals\n52: \n53: 1. This module does not submit visa applications directly to government systems in Phase 1.\n54: 2. This module does not perform live airline, hotel, or train booking in Phase 1.\n55: 3. This module does not replace the Jamaah profile.\n56: 4. This module does not replace Group Trip member assignment.\n57: 5. This module does not manage platform-wide document type master data; default document/service types are managed by platform configuration.\n58: 6. This module does not process payments; payment clearance can be displayed as read-only if needed.\n59: \n60: ## 6. Users and Roles\n61: \n62: | Role | Access Level |\n63: |---|---|\n64: | Agency Owner | Full access to all agency documents and service readiness |\n65: | Agency Admin | Manage documents, services, reminders, and bulk actions |\n66: | Operations Staff | Manage operational documents and services if permitted |\n67: | Sales Staff | View limited readiness linked to bookings they handle |\n68: | Finance Staff | View payment-related readiness only, no sensitive identity documents by default |\n69: | Mutawwif | View limited readiness for assigned trip members only |\n70: | Platform Admin | Support access only when permitted and audited |\n71: \n72: ## 7. Permission Rules\n73: \n74: | Permission | Description |\n75: |---|---|\n76: | View Document Readiness | Can view document status without opening files |\n77: | View Sensitive Documents | Can open/download IC, passport, visa, vaccination, and identity files |\n78: | Manage Documents | Can upload, replace, reject, verify, and request revision |\n79: | Manage Services | Can update visa, ticket, room, transport, kit, and other service statuses |\n80: | Bulk Update | Can update multiple records at once |\n81: | Send Reminders | Can send document/service reminder notifications |\n82: | Export Readiness | Can export document and service readiness reports |\n83: | Audit Access | Can view change logs and file access logs |\n84: \n85: Rules:\n86: \n87: 1. Sensitive document files require explicit permission.\n88: 2. Users without sensitive document permission can see status but cannot open the file.\n89: 3. Download actions must be logged.\n90: 4. Rejected documents must require a reason.\n91: 5. Bulk status changes must require confirmation.\n92: 6. Platform Admin assistance must be visible in audit logs.\n93: \n94: ## 8. Data Ownership and Scope\n95: \n96: Documents & Services follows agency-level data isolation.\n97: \n98: | Data | Ownership |\n99: |---|---|\n100: | Jamaah profile documents | Owned by jamaah, managed by agency if invited/assigned |\n101: | Booking documents | Owned by agency booking record |\n102: | Group trip documents | Owned by agency group trip operational record |\n103: | Service readiness | Owned by agency operational workflow |\n104: | Uploaded files | Stored securely outside application server storage |\n105: | Audit logs | Owned by platform and visible based on permission |\n106: \n107: Travel Agency users can only access data connected to their own agency.\n108: \n109: ## 9. Key Definitions\n110: \n111: | Term | Definition |\n112: |---|---|\n113: | Document Readiness | Completion and verification state of required travel documents |\n114: | Service Readiness | Operational completion state of required travel services |\n115: | Required Document | A document needed for a package, booking, or trip |\n116: | Optional Document | Supporting document that is useful but does not block departure |\n117: | Required Service | Service that must be completed before trip departure |\n118: | Blocking Item | Missing or rejected document/service that prevents operational readiness |\n119: | Family/Group | A grouped set of jamaah managed together under one booking/trip |\n120: | Readiness Score | Calculated percentage of complete required documents and services |\n121: \n122: ## 10. Information Architecture\n123: \n124: Documents & Services should appear as a dedicated menu in the Travel Agency Portal. It may also be deep-linked from Dashboard, Booking, Jamaah, and Group Trip pages.\n125: \n126: ```mermaid\n127: flowchart TD\n128:     DOC[\"Documents & Services\"]\n129:     DOC --> DASH[\"Readiness Dashboard\"]\n130:     DOC --> BYDOC[\"By Documents\"]\n131:     DOC --> BYSERVICE[\"By Services\"]\n132:     DOC --> MEMBER[\"By Member\"]\n133:     DOC --> TRIP[\"By Group Trip\"]\n134:     DOC --> REMINDER[\"Reminders\"]\n135:     DOC --> EXPORT[\"Export\"]\n136:     DOC --> SETTINGS[\"Document & Service Rules\"]\n137: \n138:     BYDOC --> DTYPE[\"Document Type\"]\n139:     BYDOC --> DSTATUS[\"Document Status\"]\n140:     BYSERVICE --> STYPE[\"Service Type\"]\n141:     BYSERVICE --> SSTATUS[\"Service Status\"]\n142:     TRIP --> GT[\"Group Trip Readiness\"]\n143:     MEMBER --> JM[\"Jamaah Readiness\"]\n144: ```\n145: \n146: ## 11. Navigation Entry Points\n147: \n148: | Entry Point | Behavior |\n149: |---|---|\n150: | Main menu | Opens Documents & Services dashboard |\n151: | Dashboard pending documents card | Opens filtered list by pending/rejected documents |\n152: | Dashboard service readiness card | Opens filtered list by incomplete services |\n153: | Booking details | Opens readiness filtered to selected booking |\n154: | Jamaah details | Opens readiness filtered to selected jamaah |\n155: | Group Trip details | Opens readiness filtered to selected group trip |\n156: \n157: ## 12. Readiness Lifecycle\n158: \n159: Readiness is calculated from required documents and services. Optional items can improve completeness visibility but must not block readiness unless configured as required.\n160: \n161: ```mermaid\n162: flowchart TD\n163:     START[\"Member added to booking or trip\"]\n164:     CHECK[\"System generates required checklist\"]\n165:     MISSING[\"Missing items\"]\n166:     UPLOAD[\"Document uploaded or service updated\"]\n167:     REVIEW[\"Pending review\"]\n168:     FIX[\"Revision required\"]\n169:     DONE[\"Verified or completed\"]\n170:     READY[\"Member ready\"]\n171:     BLOCK[\"Blocked\"]\n172: \n173:     START --> CHECK\n174:     CHECK --> MISSING\n175:     MISSING --> UPLOAD\n176:     UPLOAD --> REVIEW\n177:     REVIEW --> DONE\n178:     REVIEW --> FIX\n179:     FIX --> UPLOAD\n180:     MISSING --> BLOCK\n181:     DONE --> READY\n182: ```\n183: \n184: ## 13. Readiness Status Values\n185: \n186: | Status | Meaning | Blocks Readiness |\n187: |---|---|---|\n188: | Not Required | Item is not needed for this package/trip | No |\n189: | Missing | Required item has not been provided | Yes |\n190: | Uploaded | File exists but has not been reviewed | Yes |\n191: | Pending Review | Staff must verify the file/status | Yes |\n192: | Need Revision | Submitted item is invalid or incomplete | Yes |\n193: | Verified | Document has been accepted | No |\n194: | Completed | Service has been completed | No |\n195: | Expiring Soon | Document is valid but expiry is near | Warning |\n196: | Expired | Document validity has passed | Yes |\n197: | Waived | Requirement is waived with reason | No, if approved |\n198: \n199: ## 14. Readiness Calculation\n200: \n201: Readiness Score is calculated per jamaah, family/group, booking, and group trip.\n202: \n203: ```text\n204: Readiness Score = Completed Required Items / Total Required Items x 100\n205: ```\n206: \n207: Rules:\n208: \n209: 1. Optional items are excluded from the main readiness score.\n210: 2. Waived required items count as complete only when approved by authorized staff.\n211: 3. Expired required documents count as incomplete.\n212: 4. Expiring Soon documents count as complete with warning.\n213: 5. A trip is Ready only if all required members meet the required threshold.\n214: 6. Default threshold should be 100% for required items.\n215: \n216: ## 15. Main Dashboard\n217: \n218: The Documents & Services dashboard summarizes agency-wide readiness.\n219: \n220: ### 15.1 Summary Cards\n221: \n222: | Card | Description |\n223: |---|---|\n224: | Total Members Tracked | Number of jamaah included in document/service tracking |\n225: | Ready Members | Members with all required items complete |\n226: | Pending Documents | Required documents missing, pending, rejected, or expired |\n227: | Pending Services | Required services incomplete or blocked |\n228: | Expiring Soon | Documents approaching expiry |\n229: | Critical Departures | Trips departing soon with blocking items |\n230: \n231: ### 15.2 Recommended Filters\n232: \n233: | Filter | Options |\n234: |---|---|\n235: | Group Trip | All trips, selected trip |\n236: | Package | All packages, selected package |\n237: | Booking | Booking ID or customer name |\n238: | Family/Group | All, specific family/group |\n239: | Jamaah | Name, email, phone, passport number |\n240: | Document Status | Missing, Pending Review, Need Revision, Verified, Expired |\n241: | Service Status | Not Started, In Progress, Completed, Blocked |\n242: | Departure Date | All time, next 7 days, next 30 days, custom range |\n243: | Priority | Critical, warning, normal |\n244: \n245: ### 15.3 Default Sorting\n246: \n247: Default sorting should prioritize operational urgency:\n248: \n249: 1. Trips departing soonest.\n250: 2. Blocking items first.\n251: 3. Missing or rejected documents.\n252: 4. Expired or expiring soon documents.\n253: 5. Recently updated items.\n254: \n255: ## 16. By Documents View\n256: \n257: By Documents view shows each member as a row and document types as columns. It is best for seeing which jamaah still lacks travel documents.\n258: \n259: ### 16.1 Table Columns\n260: \n261: | Column | Description |\n262: |---|---|\n263: | Member | Jamaah name, email, phone, family/group marker |\n264: | Booking | Booking reference and package |\n265: | Group Trip | Assigned group trip |\n266: | IC / Identity | Status and file action |\n267: | Passport | Status, expiry, file action |\n268: | Visa | Status, application ID, file action |\n269: | Photo | Status and file action |\n270: | Vaccination | Status and file action |\n271: | Additional Documents | Medical, consent, guardian, or other custom document |\n272: | Readiness | Member document readiness |\n273: | Actions | View, upload, request revision, send reminder |\n274: \n275: ### 16.2 Document Row Behavior\n276: \n277: 1. Clicking the status opens document detail.\n278: 2. Clicking upload opens the upload modal.\n279: 3. Clicking view opens a protected preview if permission allows.\n280: 4. Missing required items are highlighted.\n281: 5. Expiring documents show warning badge and expiry date.\n282: 6. Horizontal scroll is allowed on desktop for wide tables.\n283: 7. Mobile view should collapse into member cards grouped by document type.\n284: \n285: ## 17. By Services View\n286: \n287: By Services view shows each member as a row and operational services as columns. It is best for operations staff tracking readiness before departure.\n288: \n289: ### 17.1 Table Columns\n290: \n291: | Column | Description |\n292: |---|---|\n293: | Member | Jamaah name, email, phone, family/group marker |\n294: | Booking | Booking reference and package |\n295: | Group Trip | Assigned group trip |\n296: | Visa Processing | Service status and note |\n297: | Flight E-ticket | Ticket status, class, ticket number |\n298: | Train E-ticket | Ticket status and ticket number |\n299: | Room Configuration | Room type, room number, sharing group |\n300: | Hotel Allocation | Makkah/Madinah hotel allocation |\n301: | Transport | Transport readiness |\n302: | Insurance | Insurance status |\n303: | Umrah Kit | Kit distribution status |\n304: | Briefing | Mutawwif/trip briefing status |\n305: | Readiness | Service readiness score |\n306: | Actions | Update service, bulk update, send reminder |\n307: \n308: ### 17.2 Service Status Values\n309: \n310: | Status | Meaning |\n311: |---|---|\n312: | Not Required | Service does not apply |\n313: | Not Started | Service has not been arranged |\n314: | In Progress | Service is being arranged |\n315: | Pending Confirmation | Waiting for vendor, airline, hotel, or admin confirmation |\n316: | Completed | Service is ready |\n317: | Blocked | Service cannot proceed due to missing data, document, or payment |\n318: | Cancelled | Service was cancelled and should no longer be counted |\n319: \n320: ## 18. Document Types\n321: \n322: Default document types should be configurable by package/trip rules, but Phase 1 should include the following core types.\n323: \n324: | Document Type | Required By Default | Source | Notes |\n325: |---|---|---|---|\n326: | IC / Identity Document | Yes for local jamaah | Jamaah profile | Front/back image may be required |\n327: | Passport | Yes | Jamaah profile or booking | Must track expiry date |\n328: | Passport Photo | Yes if required by visa | Jamaah profile or booking | Should follow visa photo guidelines |\n329: | Visa Document | Yes when visa is included | Booking/trip operation | Can include visa application ID |\n330: | Vaccination Certificate | Optional by default, required if package rules require it | Jamaah profile or booking | May depend on destination regulation |\n331: | Flight E-ticket | Yes when flight included | Group Trip service | Can be uploaded per member or batch |\n332: | Train E-ticket | Optional by default | Group Trip service | Required when train transport is included |\n333: | Medical Declaration | Optional | Jamaah profile or booking | Useful for elderly or special needs |\n334: | Guardian Consent | Conditional | Booking | Required for minors if applicable |\n335: | Additional Supporting Document | Optional | Booking/trip | Custom agency-specific document |\n336: \n337: ### 18.1 Requirement Type Matrix\n338: \n339: Each document or service requirement must be classified so readiness logic stays predictable.\n340: \n341: | Requirement Type | Meaning | Counts Toward Readiness | Example |\n342: |---|---|---:|---|\n343: | Required | Must be completed for the member/trip | Yes | Passport, visa document, flight e-ticket |\n344: | Optional | Nice to have, does not block readiness | No | Supporting document, extra note |\n345: | Conditional | Required only when a condition is true | Yes when condition applies | Guardian consent for minors, train ticket when train is included |\n346: | Waived | Requirement was removed by authorized user with reason | Counts as complete if approved | Visa waiver, document exception |\n347: | Not Required | Requirement does not apply | No | Train ticket for package without train |\n348: \n349: Rules:\n350: - Package and Group Trip should generate the default requirement checklist.\n351: - Agency staff may request a waiver only if they have permission.\n352: - Waived items must store reason, approver, timestamp, and expiry if temporary.\n353: - A requirement cannot be changed from Required to Optional after trip activation without impact review.\n354: \n355: ## 19. Service Types\n356: \n357: Default service types are linked to Package and Group Trip setup.\n358: \n359: | Service Type | Required When | Notes |\n360: |---|---|---|\n361: | Visa Processing | Package includes visa service | Can be blocked by missing passport/photo |\n362: | Flight Ticket | Package includes flight | Can be updated per member |\n363: | Train Ticket | Package includes train/inter-city rail | Optional unless selected in package/trip |\n364: | Hotel Allocation | Package includes hotel | Tracks assigned hotel per city |\n365: | Room Configuration | Room pricing/assignment exists | Tracks room type and room number |\n366: | Transport | Package/trip includes transport | Airport, Makkah, Madinah, inter-city |\n367: | Insurance | Package includes insurance | May require policy number |\n368: | Umrah Kit | Agency provides kit | Tracks distributed/not distributed |\n369: | Briefing | Trip requires briefing | Tracks briefing attendance |\n370: | Special Request | Member has dietary, wheelchair, medical, or other request | Can be optional or required |\n371: \n372: ## 20. Document Upload Rules\n373: \n374: Uploads must be controlled to prevent performance and storage issues.\n375: \n376: ### 20.1 File Type and Size Limits\n377: \n378: | Upload Type | Allowed Formats | Max Size | Notes |\n379: |---|---|---:|---|\n380: | Profile image | JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP | 2 MB | Compress and crop before upload |\n381: | IC / Identity image | JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP, PDF | 3 MB | Prefer image under 2 MB |\n382: | Passport scan | JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP, PDF | 5 MB | Must be readable |\n383: | Passport photo | JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP | 2 MB | Should be portrait image |\n384: | Visa document | JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP, PDF | 5 MB | Store application ID if available |\n385: | Vaccination document | JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP, PDF | 5 MB | Must support preview |\n386: | E-ticket flight | PDF, JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP | 5 MB | PDF preferred |\n387: | E-ticket train | PDF, JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP | 5 MB | PDF preferred |\n388: | Medical/consent document | PDF, JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP | 5 MB | Sensitive document |\n389: | Bulk ZIP upload | ZIP | 25 MB | Optional Phase 2 unless needed |\n390: \n391: ### 20.2 Server Load Rules\n392: \n393: 1. Image files should be compressed client-side before upload when possible.\n394: 2. Files must be uploaded directly to object storage through signed upload URLs.\n395: 3. Application server should store metadata only, not raw file blobs.\n396: 4. Thumbnails should be generated asynchronously.\n397: 5. Preview should use optimized preview URLs, not original files.\n398: 6. Large files must not be loaded inside list tables.\n399: 7. Files must be virus-scanned before being marked available.\n400: 8. Failed uploads should not create completed document records.\n401: 9. Duplicate uploads should warn staff before replacing an existing file.\n402: 10. Old file versions should be retained only based on retention policy.\n403: \n404: ### 20.3 Duplicate File and Expiry Rules\n405: \n406: 1. Duplicate detection should compare filename, size, checksum, document type, member, and booking/trip context.\n407: 2. If duplicate is detected, staff can keep existing, replace existing, or upload as new version if permitted.\n408: 3. Passport, visa, vaccination, insurance, and other expiry-based documents must store expiry date when available.\n409: 4. Expiry warning should be configurable by document type, with default warning at 6 months for passport and 30 days for short-term travel documents.\n410: 5. Expired required documents must block readiness unless waived.\n411: 6. Document preview must use optimized files; original file download requires explicit permission and audit log.\n412: \n413: ## 21. Document Review Flow\n414: \n415: ```mermaid\n416: flowchart TD\n417:     AGENCY[\"Agency staff opens missing document\"]\n418:     UPLOAD[\"Upload or replace file\"]\n419:     VALIDATE[\"System validates format and size\"]\n420:     SCAN[\"Virus scan and metadata extraction\"]\n421:     REVIEW[\"Pending review\"]\n422:     DECISION{\"Review decision\"}\n423:     VERIFY[\"Mark as verified\"]\n424:     REVISION[\"Request revision with reason\"]\n425:     REJECT[\"Reject with reason\"]\n426:     NOTIFY[\"Notify jamaah or responsible staff\"]\n427:     LOG[\"Save audit log\"]\n428: \n429:     AGENCY --> UPLOAD\n430:     UPLOAD --> VALIDATE\n431:     VALIDATE --> SCAN\n432:     SCAN --> REVIEW\n433:     REVIEW --> DECISION\n434:     DECISION --> VERIFY\n435:     DECISION --> REVISION\n436:     DECISION --> REJECT\n437:     REVISION --> NOTIFY\n438:     REJECT --> NOTIFY\n439:     VERIFY --> LOG\n440:     NOTIFY --> LOG\n441: ```\n442: \n443: ## 22. Service Update Flow\n444: \n445: ```mermaid\n446: flowchart TD\n447:     STAFF[\"Agency staff opens service item\"]\n448:     UPDATE[\"Update service status\"]\n449:     DETAIL[\"Add service details\"]\n450:     CHECK[\"System checks dependent data\"]\n451:     BLOCKED[\"Blocked if dependency missing\"]\n452:     COMPLETE[\"Completed\"]\n453:     SYNC[\"Sync to booking and group trip readiness\"]\n454:     LOG[\"Save audit log\"]\n455: \n456:     STAFF --> UPDATE\n457:     UPDATE --> DETAIL\n458:     DETAIL --> CHECK\n459:     CHECK --> BLOCKED\n460:     CHECK --> COMPLETE\n461:     BLOCKED --> SYNC\n462:     COMPLETE --> SYNC\n463:     SYNC --> LOG\n464: ```\n465: \n466: ## 23. Dependency Rules\n467: \n468: | Dependency | Rule |\n469: |---|---|\n470: | Visa Processing depends on Passport | Visa cannot be completed if passport is missing or expired |\n471: | Visa Processing depends on Passport Photo | Visa cannot be completed if required photo is missing |\n472: | Flight E-ticket depends on Flight Selection | Ticket service should only appear if package/trip includes flight |\n473: | Train E-ticket depends on Train Selection | Ticket service should only appear if trip includes train |\n474: | Room Configuration depends on Hotel Selection | Room assignment requires selected hotel and room rules |\n475: | Briefing depends on Group Trip Assignment | Briefing service is linked to assigned group trip |\n476: | Insurance depends on package inclusion | Insurance appears only if included in package/service setup |\n477: | Payment Clearance depends on Finance | Payment readiness is read-only and sourced from billing/finance |\n478: \n479: ## 24. Upload / Update Document Form\n480: \n481: | Field | Type | Required | Validation | Notes |\n482: |---|---|---|---|---|\n483: | Member | Read-only selector | Yes | Must belong to agency | Pre-filled from current context |\n484: | Booking | Read-only selector | Conditional | Required for booking-specific document | Pre-filled if opened from booking |\n485: | Group Trip | Read-only selector | Conditional | Required for trip-specific document | Pre-filled if opened from trip |\n486: | Document Type | Dropdown | Yes | Must be allowed for package/trip | Passport, Visa, Photo, etc. |\n487: | Requirement Type | Dropdown | Yes | Required, Optional, Conditional | Defaults from package/trip rules |\n488: | File | Upload | Conditional | Format and size limits apply | Required unless only updating metadata |\n489: | Document Number | Text | Conditional | Max 80 chars | Passport number, visa ID, etc. |\n490: | Issue Date | Date | Optional | Must be valid date | Useful for passport/visa |\n491: | Expiry Date | Date | Conditional | Must be after issue date | Required for passport/visa if available |\n492: | Status | Dropdown | Yes | Valid status only | Missing, Pending, Verified, etc. |\n493: | Review Note | Textarea | Conditional | Required when Need Revision or Rejected | Visible based on visibility setting |\n494: | Visibility | Dropdown | Yes | Internal, Jamaah Visible, Agency Only | Default Agency Only for sensitive data |\n495: | Notify Jamaah | Checkbox | Optional | Boolean | Sends update if allowed |\n496: \n497: ## 25. Service Update Form\n498: \n499: | Field | Type | Required | Validation | Notes |\n500: |---|---|---|---|---|\n501: | Member | Read-only selector | Yes | Must belong to agency | Pre-filled |\n502: | Booking | Read-only selector | Yes | Must belong to agency | Pre-filled |\n503: | Group Trip | Read-only selector | Conditional | Required for trip service | Pre-filled when relevant |\n504: | Service Type | Dropdown | Yes | Must be configured for package/trip | Visa, flight, room, transport, etc. |\n505: | Status | Dropdown | Yes | Valid service status | Not Started, In Progress, Completed, Blocked |\n506: | Service Reference | Text | Optional | Max 100 chars | Ticket number, insurance policy, room number |\n507: | Assigned Vendor | Dropdown/text | Optional | Max 120 chars | Airline, train, hotel, transport vendor |\n508: | Date / Time | Date/time | Optional | Valid date/time | Useful for ticket or appointment |\n509: | Notes | Textarea | Optional | Max 500 chars | Internal operational note |\n510: | Attachment | Upload | Optional | File limits apply | Useful for ticket, voucher, policy |\n511: | Notify Jamaah | Checkbox | Optional | Boolean | Sends service update notification |\n512: \n513: ## 26. Bulk Status Update Form\n514: \n515: | Field | Type | Required | Validation | Notes |\n516: |---|---|---|---|---|\n517: | Selected Members | Multi-select | Yes | At least 1 selected | From current table selection |\n518: | Update Type | Dropdown | Yes | Document or Service | Determines next fields |\n519: | Document/Service Type | Dropdown | Yes | Must apply to selected records | Example Passport, Visa, Flight Ticket |\n520: | New Status | Dropdown | Yes | Valid transition only | Prevent invalid jumps if configured |\n521: | Reason / Note | Textarea | Conditional | Required for rejection, waiver, blocked | Stored in audit log |\n522: | Notify Affected Users | Checkbox | Optional | Boolean | Sends reminder/update |\n523: | Confirmation | Checkbox | Yes | Must confirm | Prevent accidental mass changes |\n524: \n525: ## 27. Reminder Form\n526: \n527: | Field | Type | Required | Validation | Notes |\n528: |---|---|---|---|---|\n529: | Recipient Type | Dropdown | Yes | Jamaah, Family PIC, Internal Staff | Defaults based on context |\n530: | Recipients | Multi-select | Yes | At least 1 | Can include selected members |\n531: | Reminder Reason | Dropdown | Yes | Missing document, rejected document, pending service, expiring soon | Used for template |\n532: | Message | Textarea | Yes | Max 1000 chars | Template can be edited |\n533: | Channel | Checkbox group | Yes | Email, WhatsApp, in-app | Based on notification settings |\n534: | Send Now / Schedule | Radio | Yes | Valid schedule | Phase 1 can support Send Now only |\n535: \n536: ## 28. Waiver Rules\n537: \n538: Waiver allows an agency to mark a required item as not blocking readiness.\n539: \n540: Rules:\n541: \n542: 1. Waiver requires permission.\n543: 2. Waiver requires reason.\n544: 3. Waiver must be visible in audit log.\n545: 4. Waived item should show special badge.\n546: 5. Platform Admin may need visibility for compliance-sensitive waivers.\n547: 6. Waiver should not be used for expired passport or mandatory legal document unless platform policy allows it.\n548: \n549: ## 29. Notifications\n550: \n551: | Event | Recipient | Channel |\n552: |---|---|---|\n553: | Document requested | Jamaah or Family PIC | Email, WhatsApp, in-app |\n554: | Document rejected | Jamaah or Family PIC | Email, WhatsApp, in-app |\n555: | Document verified | Internal staff, optional jamaah | In-app, optional email |\n556: | Document expiring soon | Agency staff, jamaah | In-app, email, WhatsApp |\n557: | Service completed | Agency staff, jamaah if customer-facing | In-app, optional email |\n558: | Service blocked | Agency operations staff | In-app |\n559: | Trip readiness critical | Agency owner/admin/operations | In-app, email |\n560: \n561: Notification messages must avoid exposing sensitive document content.\n562: \n563: ## 30. Export\n564: \n565: Export should help operations teams prepare departure checks.\n566: \n567: ### 30.1 Export Formats\n568: \n569: | Format | Phase | Notes |\n570: |---|---|---|\n571: | CSV | Phase 1 | Good for operations spreadsheet |\n572: | PDF | Phase 1 | Good for management summary |\n573: | XLSX | Phase 2 | Useful for bulk operations |\n574: \n575: ### 30.2 Export Rules\n576: \n577: 1. Export must respect role permissions.\n578: 2. Sensitive document numbers should be masked unless user has permission.\n579: 3. File download links should not be included by default.\n580: 4. Export should include generated date, agency name, filters, and user who exported.\n581: 5. Export action must be audited.\n582: \n583: ## 31. Functional Requirements\n584: \n585: | ID | Requirement | Priority |\n586: |---|---|---|\n587: | TA-DOC-001 | System must allow agency users to view only documents and services under their own agency. | P0 |\n588: | TA-DOC-002 | System must provide a Documents & Services dashboard with readiness summary. | P0 |\n589: | TA-DOC-003 | System must support filtering by trip, package, booking, jamaah, family/group, document status, service status, and departure date. | P0 |\n590: | TA-DOC-004 | System must provide a By Documents view. | P0 |\n591: | TA-DOC-005 | System must provide a By Services view. | P0 |\n592: | TA-DOC-006 | System must support protected upload, preview, replace, and download actions for documents. | P0 |\n593: | TA-DOC-007 | System must enforce upload format and max-size rules. | P0 |\n594: | TA-DOC-008 | System must store files outside application server storage and keep metadata in the application database. | P0 |\n595: | TA-DOC-009 | System must support document statuses: Missing, Uploaded, Pending Review, Need Revision, Verified, Expiring Soon, Expired, Waived. | P0 |\n596: | TA-DOC-010 | System must support service statuses: Not Required, Not Started, In Progress, Pending Confirmation, Completed, Blocked, Cancelled. | P0 |\n597: | TA-DOC-011 | System must calculate readiness score per member, booking, family/group, and group trip. | P0 |\n598: | TA-DOC-012 | System must sync readiness status with Booking and Group Trip modules. | P0 |\n599: | TA-DOC-013 | System must require a reason when rejecting documents, requesting revision, marking blocked, or waiving requirement. | P0 |\n600: | TA-DOC-014 | System must support reminders for missing, rejected, pending, and expiring documents. | P1 |\n601: | TA-DOC-015 | System must support bulk status update with confirmation. | P1 |\n602: | TA-DOC-016 | System must protect sensitive document file access by permission. | P0 |\n603: | TA-DOC-017 | System must log upload, preview, download, replacement, status change, reminder, export, and waiver actions. | P0 |\n604: | TA-DOC-018 | System must support document expiry date warnings. | P1 |\n605: | TA-DOC-019 | System must support member cards on mobile for wide document/service tables. | P1 |\n606: | TA-DOC-020 | System must support export of readiness report based on user permission. | P1 |\n607: | TA-DOC-021 | System must show blocked dependencies, such as visa blocked by missing passport. | P1 |\n608: | TA-DOC-022 | System must show payment clearance as read-only when used as readiness signal. | P2 |\n609: | TA-DOC-023 | System should support batch e-ticket upload mapping in Phase 2. | P2 |\n610: | TA-DOC-024 | System should support OCR-assisted document metadata extraction in Phase 2. | P2 |\n611: \n612: ## 32. Business Rules\n613: \n614: 1. A member can have multiple document records across different booking/trip contexts.\n615: 2. Profile-level documents can be reused, but trip-specific documents must keep trip context.\n616: 3. A replaced document should not delete the old file immediately if retention policy requires history.\n617: 4. Staff cannot mark a missing required document as Verified without a file unless waiver permission is used.\n618: 5. Expired documents cannot be marked Verified.\n619: 6. Service completion can require supporting attachment if configured.\n620: 7. A cancelled member should be excluded from active readiness counts.\n621: 8. Group Trip readiness must update when member document or service status changes.\n622: 9. Sensitive fields should be masked in list views when permission is limited.\n623: 10. Mutawwif users must not download sensitive documents unless explicitly permitted.\n624: \n625: ## 33. Empty, Loading, and Error States\n626: \n627: | State | Behavior |\n628: |---|---|\n629: | Empty dashboard | Show message that no booking or trip members are available yet |\n630: | No filter result | Show reset filters action |\n631: | Upload in progress | Show file name, progress, and cancel option |\n632: | Upload too large | Show max file size and compression suggestion |\n633: | Unsupported format | Show allowed formats |\n634: | Virus scan failed | Block file, show upload failed status, notify staff |\n635: | Permission denied | Show status-only view and explain access is restricted |\n636: | Sync delayed | Show last synced timestamp |\n637: | Export failed | Show retry option and keep filters |\n638: \n639: ## 34. Responsive Behavior\n640: \n641: | Device | Behavior |\n642: |---|---|\n643: | Desktop | Full table with horizontal scroll for many document/service columns |\n644: | Tablet | Condensed table with sticky member column and grouped columns |\n645: | Mobile | Member-card layout grouped by Documents and Services |\n646: \n647: Mobile rules:\n648: \n649: 1. Filters should collapse into filter drawer.\n650: 2. Each member card should show readiness score first.\n651: 3. Required missing items should appear above optional items.\n652: 4. Upload actions should remain reachable but not crowd the card.\n653: 5. Sensitive document preview should require permission confirmation.\n654: \n655: ## 35. Audit Log\n656: \n657: Each sensitive or operational action must create audit history.\n658: \n659: | Action | Audit Fields |\n660: |---|---|\n661: | Upload file | User, timestamp, member, document type, file metadata |\n662: | Replace file | User, timestamp, old file reference, new file reference |\n663: | Preview file | User, timestamp, file ID |\n664: | Download file | User, timestamp, file ID, IP/device metadata if available |\n665: | Change status | User, old status, new status, reason |\n666: | Send reminder | User, recipient, channel, template, timestamp |\n667: | Bulk update | User, affected record count, new status, reason |\n668: | Export | User, filters, format, timestamp |\n669: | Waive requirement | User, reason, affected item |\n670: \n671: ## 36. Security and Privacy\n672: \n673: 1. All document files must use private storage.\n674: 2. File access must use expiring signed URLs.\n675: 3. Sensitive documents should not be cached publicly.\n676: 4. File preview and download must require authentication.\n677: 5. Personally identifiable information must be masked based on permission.\n678: 6. Upload files must be scanned for malware.\n679: 7. Access logs must be retained based on platform policy.\n680: 8. User sessions should be checked before file preview/download.\n681: 9. Direct file URLs must not expose storage bucket paths.\n682: 10. Deleted/archived records should not immediately delete compliance-sensitive files unless retention policy allows it.\n683: \n684: ## 37. Data Model - Product Level\n685: \n686: ```mermaid\n687: flowchart LR\n688:     AGENCY[\"Travel Agency\"]\n689:     BOOKING[\"Booking\"]\n690:     TRIP[\"Group Trip\"]\n691:     MEMBER[\"Trip Member\"]\n692:     JAMAAH[\"Jamaah\"]\n693:     DOCREQ[\"Document Requirement\"]\n694:     DOCREC[\"Document Record\"]\n695:     SERVREQ[\"Service Requirement\"]\n696:     SERVREC[\"Service Record\"]\n697:     FILE[\"File Asset\"]\n698:     AUDIT[\"Audit Log\"]\n699: \n700:     AGENCY --> BOOKING\n701:     AGENCY --> TRIP\n702:     BOOKING --> MEMBER\n703:     TRIP --> MEMBER\n704:     MEMBER --> JAMAAH\n705:     MEMBER --> DOCREC\n706:     MEMBER --> SERVREC\n707:     DOCREQ --> DOCREC\n708:     SERVREQ --> SERVREC\n709:     DOCREC --> FILE\n710:     SERVREC --> FILE\n711:     DOCREC --> AUDIT\n712:     SERVREC --> AUDIT\n713: ```\n714: \n715: ## 38. Data Fields - Document Record\n716: \n717: | Field | Description |\n718: |---|---|\n719: | document_record_id | Unique document record ID |\n720: | agency_id | Owning agency |\n721: | jamaah_id | Linked jamaah |\n722: | booking_id | Linked booking, if applicable |\n723: | group_trip_id | Linked group trip, if applicable |\n724: | document_type | Passport, visa, vaccination, etc. |\n725: | requirement_type | Required, optional, conditional |\n726: | status | Current document status |\n727: | document_number | Passport number, visa ID, etc. |\n728: | issue_date | Issue date if applicable |\n729: | expiry_date | Expiry date if applicable |\n730: | file_asset_id | Linked uploaded file |\n731: | review_note | Reviewer note |\n732: | visibility | Internal, jamaah visible, agency only |\n733: | last_updated_by | User who last updated |\n734: | last_updated_at | Timestamp |\n735: \n736: ## 39. Data Fields - Service Record\n737: \n738: | Field | Description |\n739: |---|---|\n740: | service_record_id | Unique service record ID |\n741: | agency_id | Owning agency |\n742: | jamaah_id | Linked jamaah |\n743: | booking_id | Linked booking |\n744: | group_trip_id | Linked group trip |\n745: | service_type | Visa, flight, room, transport, etc. |\n746: | requirement_type | Required, optional, conditional |\n747: | status | Current service status |\n748: | service_reference | Ticket number, room number, policy number, etc. |\n749: | assigned_vendor | Airline, hotel, transport provider |\n750: | service_datetime | Related date/time |\n751: | attachment_file_id | Optional supporting file |\n752: | notes | Internal notes |\n753: | last_updated_by | User who last updated |\n754: | last_updated_at | Timestamp |\n755: \n756: ## 40. Acceptance Criteria\n757: \n758: 1. Agency users can open Documents & Services and only see their own agency records.\n759: 2. Dashboard shows document and service readiness summary.\n760: 3. By Documents view shows member-level document status by type.\n761: 4. By Services view shows member-level service status by type.\n762: 5. Upload form enforces allowed formats and max file sizes.\n763: 6. Oversized files are blocked before upload when possible.\n764: 7. Sensitive document preview/download is permission-protected.\n765: 8. Rejection, revision request, blocked status, and waiver require reason.\n766: 9. Readiness score updates when document/service status changes.\n767: 10. Group Trip readiness receives updated member readiness.\n768: 11. Reminder can be sent for selected missing/rejected/pending items.\n769: 12. Bulk update requires confirmation and creates audit log.\n770: 13. Export respects permissions and masks sensitive data when needed.\n771: 14. Mobile layout remains usable without wide-table overflow as primary interaction.\n772: \n773: ## 41. Recommended Phase Scope\n774: \n775: ### Phase 1\n776: \n777: 1. Documents & Services dashboard.\n778: 2. By Documents view.\n779: 3. By Services view.\n780: 4. Document upload, preview, replace, and status update.\n781: 5. Service status update.\n782: 6. Readiness score.\n783: 7. Reminder send now.\n784: 8. Export CSV/PDF summary.\n785: 9. Audit log.\n786: 10. Upload size and format enforcement.\n787: \n788: ### Phase 2\n789: \n790: 1. Bulk ZIP upload with member mapping.\n791: 2. OCR-assisted document metadata extraction.\n792: 3. Advanced document requirement builder.\n793: 4. Automated expiry reminder schedule.\n794: 5. Vendor/API integration for visa, airline, hotel, or train updates.\n795: 6. XLSX export.\n796: 7.
